macro_summary: Markets extended their recovery with SPY gaining 0.77% to $699.94 and QQQ up 1.34% to $637.40, as VIX compressed further to 18.17 — its lowest level in weeks — signaling sustained risk-on sentiment. The US-Iran Hormuz blockade enters day 4 with WTI holding at $91.20, well below the $104 single-session peak seen last week, as markets appear to be pricing the disruption as contained rather than escalating. Q1 earnings season is accelerating, with TSMC reporting today (April 16) — per Reuters, the company is expected to post a 50% quarterly profit surge driven by insatiable AI chip demand, which will be the key read-through for the semis cluster. The Fed held rates at 3.50%-3.75% per the digest, and with US10Y at 4.28% — still above the 4.20% threshold that would ease NIM pressure on Singapore banks — the rate backdrop remains a headwind for the Singapore cluster.
commodities_note: WTI at $91.20 (+0.29%) remains roughly $13 below its blockade-day spike high, suggesting markets are partially discounting a near-term Hormuz resolution — but with the blockade now entering day 4 and Iran's parliamentary speaker warning Americans will be 'nostalgic for $4-$5 gas' per CNN, the geopolitical premium remains structurally embedded. Gold at $4,844.50 (-0.11%) is consolidating near all-time highs — no attractive entry exists given the extreme extension above its 50-day average; GLD is not an actionable setup at current levels. The DXY at 98.02 (-0.14%) continues its mild softening trend, which mildly supports non-US earnings translations for multinationals.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. DBS sits 1.7% above its 50-day average of SGD 56.41, which is the tight invalidation level — normal intraday volatility could test this stop, so use the structural support at SGD 52.75 as the mental floor for position sizing. The headline R:R of 1.5:1 to the SGD 58.78 resistance is below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE, and the sizing R:R to structural support is a poor 0.3:1, reflecting how far the stock has already moved from its base. Volume is below average, which argues against a full-conviction entry. The fundamental picture is soft: forward PE of 13.9x sits 9.4% above the Singapore cluster median of 12.7x, and analyst consensus expects an 8.8% earnings decline — a meaningful headwind that's hard to ignore. US10Y at 4.28% remains above the 4.20% threshold where NIM pressure begins to ease, and the MAS maintained its SGD appreciation pace on April 14, creating a dual squeeze on funding costs and FX. Nearest resistance at SGD 57.47 is just 0.2% away — effectively at current price. Bottom line: DBS offers a decent 5.3% yield but you're paying a slight premium to peers for a bank with declining earnings in a persistently high-rate environment. Wait for US10Y to break sustainably below 4.20% before adding, or for a pullback to the SGD 56.30-56.41 support zone with volume.

O39_SI.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.8% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=17 (<30), RSI 68 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — OCBC has surged 5.81% above its 50-day average of SGD 21.53, triggering the hard block (>5% extension), and RSI at 68 is approaching overbought territory. The entry block is confirmed: fewer than 30 days above the SMA, RSI above 55, and the SMA50 direction (while rising) does not qualify for a sustained trend exception at this stage. The R:R is unfavourable at 0.9:1 (derived from SMA levels, not observed swing points) — downside to the 50-day average is 5.5% versus upside to resistance of 5.0%. Volume was below average on the recent rally, reducing conviction. OCBC's fundamental profile is the best of the Singapore trio — forward PE at 12.7x is exactly in line with the cluster median, 3.67% yield, and 2.7% earnings growth — but this is the wrong price to enter given the extension. The NIM compression narrative (US10Y 4.28%, above the 4.20% reset threshold) hasn't resolved. Bottom line: OCBC is the most fundamentally sound Singapore bank on the watchlist, but it's extended after a strong run — avoid at current levels and wait for a pullback toward SGD 21.50-22.16 before reconsidering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from ACCUMULATE to WATCH — the rr_distorted flag is true (headline R:R of 8.8:1 is driven by a 0.6% stop to the 50-day average, which normal intraday volatility can breach, making it mathematically meaningless). Gate 8 fails, blocking ACCUMULATE. The actionable sizing R:R using structural support at SGD 35.92 as the stop is only 1.3:1 — below the 2.0 threshold. UOB at SGD 37.49 is just 0.65% above its 50-day average of SGD 37.25, which puts it in the entry zone technically, but with a near-zero stop it requires structural support sizing. Analyst consensus is HOLD with a 6.7% expected earnings decline — a real headwind. Forward PE of 10.3x is 18.9% below the cluster median, offering a relative discount, and the 3.78% yield provides income support. The 50-day SMA is flat (not rising), and the NIM compression thesis (US10Y 4.28% > 4.20%) hasn't resolved — MAS maintained its SGD appreciation pace on April 14. Bottom line: UOB is the cheapest of the three Singapore banks relative to peers, but with declining earnings and no rate relief in sight, the discount is warranted. Only becomes actionable if US10Y breaks below 4.20% sustainably, or on a pullback to the SGD 35.92 support zone.

NVDA.entry_block: BLOCKED: +9.0% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=5 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 70 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVIDIA is on an 11-session win streak per Investor's Business Daily, but at $198.87 it's now 9.0% above its 50-day average of $182.38 with RSI at 69.8 — the entry block is hard (>5% extension, <30 days above SMA, SMA not rising). Volume confirms the move, which is notable, but the R:R is unfavourable at 0.8:1: downside to the $182.38 invalidation is 8.3% versus 6.7% upside to the $212.17 resistance. The fundamental case remains compelling — 17.7x forward PE is 22.7% below the semis cluster median, PEG of 0.80 with 73.2% revenue growth and 95.6% expected earnings growth is exceptional value on a growth-adjusted basis. The Blackwell Ultra ramp (per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings call) and the $78B Q1 guide remain the structural drivers. A Benzinga piece today flagged gallium supply risk as a materials-side vulnerability worth monitoring — GaN is central to next-gen datacenter power systems, and gallium has few substitutes if supply tightens. No new entry at current levels. Support at $177.41 on a pullback would reframe the setup.

AMD.entry_block: BLOCKED: +23.0% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD at $258.12 is an extreme 23.0% above its 50-day average of $209.85, triggering the hard block — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of any fundamental argument. RSI at 74.8 is deep in overbought territory. The R:R at current price is 0.2:1 — 18.7% downside to the 50-day average versus 3.0% upside to the $265.85 resistance. The fundamental thesis is strong: the Meta GPU supply framework agreement (up to 6GW with MI450, per AMD IR and confirmed by CNBC) validates AMD as a credible hyperscale alternative to NVIDIA — but this is a framework ceiling, not a firm purchase order, and revenue is contingent on MI450 benchmarks. PEG of 0.76 with 34.1% revenue growth at 23.8x forward PE is genuinely attractive. Motley Fool coverage today notes MI450 could be a significant upside catalyst. Volume has not confirmed the recent surge. This is a strong thesis at the wrong price — wait for a pullback toward the $224.64 support zone before this becomes a WATCH candidate.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 26% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel at $64.94 is a remarkable 34.7% above its 50-day average — the most extended name on the watchlist — and entry is hard-blocked at any level without a crash-level retracement. RSI at 75.1 and the 1-month return of 41.9% signals exhaustion risk. The R:R from current price is 0.2:1 (5.0% upside to $68.19 resistance, 25.8% downside to the SMA50). Forward PE of 62.3x is 172% above the semis cluster median — the most expensive semi on a PE basis — with negative revenue growth (-4.1%) and negative FCF yield (-1.38%). The 18A foundry thesis remains unproven: as of April 2026, there are no confirmed external customers, only internal Panther Lake production. High-NA EUV customer ramp with Intel 14A is an H2 2026 event per the ASML earnings call — meaningful but not yet a revenue catalyst. The SMA50 is 26% below price, making any SMA-based entry guidance irrelevant; use the $40.63 structural support as the reference if this collapses. No entry case exists today — this is a speculative sentiment-driven run that requires significant mean reversion before becoming actionable.

ASML.entry_block: BLOCKED: +6.1% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), RSI 60 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML fell 3.05% after reporting Q1 earnings: per Investor's Business Daily, the company beat Q1 estimates but missed with its Q2 sales outlook, sending the stock down. At $1,481.77, it's 6.1% above its 50-day average of $1,396.03, still within the hard block zone (>5% extension, RSI 59.6 above 55, fewer than 30 days above SMA). The R:R is 0.8:1 — unfavourable — though note that the pipeline flagged tight_upside: nearest resistance is at $1,491.51 (just 0.7% away), with the real ceiling at $1,547.22 (4.4% upside). Volume confirmed the move. PEG of 2.49 flags expensive-for-growth, with revenue growth of only 4.9% against a 32.4x forward PE — 41.5% above the semis cluster median. FCF yield of 1.87% is thin. The $71B 2030 revenue target requires EUV adoption to remain on schedule across TSMC, Samsung, and Intel — the Hormuz blockade logistics headwinds compound pressure on that timeline. Structural support at $1,314.33 is 11.3% below current price — the 200-day SMA at $1,076.17 is the deeper floor. A pullback to $1,382.75-$1,396.03 would be the first level worth watching for re-entry.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +12.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), RSI 63 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Micron at $456.23 is 12.9% above its 50-day average of $404.03, hard-blocked with RSI at 63.2. The stock pulled back 1.07% today after a strong April rally, with Yahoo Finance noting profit-taking. The R:R at current price is a poor 0.3:1 — 11.4% downside to the SMA50 versus 3.3% upside to the $471.14 resistance. The valuation picture is compelling: forward PE of 4.6x (vs. cluster median of 22.9x) represents an 79.9% discount — the cheapest semi on a forward earnings basis. However, the PEG of 0.27 warrants a flag: with forward PE at 4.6x and such a low PEG, the implied earnings growth rate appears extraordinarily high — the 756% expected earnings growth figure is real but reflects a cyclical trough reversal, not a structural re-rating. UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri raised his target to $535 (from $510) citing a durable memory pricing upcycle — a bullish call, but note Micron's own management projections on memory prices carry inherent seller bias. The inference-driven HBM demand thesis (KV cache scaling) remains intact. Structural support at $363.75 is 20.3% below — that's the level where a genuine re-entry case begins to build on a pullback.

TSM.entry_block: BLOCKED: +6.5% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), RSI 63 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION — and TSMC reports earnings today (April 16), within the 7-day earnings proximity window, which adds binary risk to any entry attempt. Per Reuters, TSMC is expected to post a 50% quarterly profit surge on AI demand, with 3nm production and CoWoS packaging running above capacity. At $375.10, TSMC is 6.5% above its 50-day average of $352.25, hard-blocked (>5% extension, 6 days above SMA, RSI 62.7). The earnings report could resolve the CAUTION signal if management confirms CoWoS capacity expansion and pricing power — or deepen it if capex guidance disappoints. R:R at current price is 0.6:1 (tight_upside applies: nearest resistance is $378.93 at 1.0% above, with the meaningful ceiling at $389.11 at 3.7% above). Volume confirmed the recent move. Forward PE of 20.3x at PEG 1.34 is reasonable given 20.5% revenue growth and analyst consensus expects 35% earnings growth. If TSMC confirms strong Q2 guidance today, a pullback to $352-$363 zone would represent a clean WATCH re-entry with improving R:R. Structural support at $330.99 is 11.8% below.

AMZN.entry_block: BLOCKED: +16.3% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 76 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Amazon at $248.50 is 16.3% above its 50-day average of $213.67 — hard-blocked and deep in extension territory with RSI at 75.6. The $200B 2026 capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings and 10-K) is the structural thesis, but that level of spending sent shares down 10% on Q1 operating income concerns — the market has already partially re-priced for it. Earnings on April 30 are the key binary catalyst: AWS revenue trajectory and capex-to-earnings conversion timeline are the pivotal metrics. The R:R at current price is 0.3:1 — deeply unfavourable. Forward PE of 26.4x at PEG 1.91 is fairly valued but not compelling entry. FCF yield of 0.89% is thin for a cash investor. Lumen struck a connectivity deal with AWS today per Barron's, signaling ongoing cloud infrastructure demand — directionally positive but immaterial to the entry case. The stock needs to revert to the $225.96-$213.67 zone before the setup improves.

GOOG.entry_block: BLOCKED: +8.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=6 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 72 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Alphabet at $334.47 is 8.95% above its 50-day average of $307.00 — hard-blocked with RSI at 71.9. The R:R is a poor 0.2:1 (8.2% downside to SMA50 invalidation vs. 1.9% upside to nearest resistance at $340.96). April 30 earnings are the binary catalyst — Q1 cloud growth, TPU v7 rental revenue (Meta's multi-year deal is the first hyperscale TPU deployment outside Google Cloud, per The Information), and capex guidance will be closely watched. The Aptoide antitrust suit and mass advertiser arbitration actions per Simply Wall St. are incremental legal risk, not thesis-changers. PEG of 1.95 at 24.9x forward PE with 18% revenue growth and 31.1% expected earnings growth is fairly valued. OpenAI concentration is less of a concern for Google than for Microsoft — the TPU licensing model is cleaner. GitLab running AI Gateway on Google Cloud (Motley Fool) is a minor positive signal for enterprise AI adoption. No entry case until the stock pulls back to $308-$319 support zone.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — Microsoft surged 4.26% today to $411.22, triggering the RSI hard block (RSI 67.3 > 65 threshold). The pipeline correctly pre-computed this block. MSFT is now 4.98% above its 50-day average of $391.71, approaching the 5% soft extension zone. The move was driven by risk-on sentiment around potential US-Iran peace negotiations per StockStory. Volume confirmed the move. R:R is unfavourable at 0.6:1 — tight_upside applies: nearest resistance is $413.05 (0.4% above), with the real ceiling at $422.72 (2.8% above), versus 4.7% downside to invalidation at $391.71. April 30 earnings are the key catalyst — focus should be on non-OpenAI Azure RPO growth (the ~$350B figure, up 28%) and Azure ex-OpenAI commentary, since ~45% of the $625B commercial RPO is tied to OpenAI, creating headline inflation risk in Azure growth numbers. PEG of 1.25 with 16.7% revenue growth at 21.8x forward PE is attractive, and the 59.8% expected earnings growth implies a significant inflection. FCF yield of 1.76% is thin but manageable. Bottom line: Microsoft has one of the cleaner valuation setups in BigTech, but today's RSI surge blocks entry — wait for a pullback to $391-$395 around the 50-day average before April 30 earnings provide the real entry signal.

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom at $396.72 is a 20.4% extreme extension above its 50-day average of $329.44 — hard-blocked at this level with RSI at 77.0. Meta extended its custom AI chip partnership with Broadcom per StockStory, which reinforces the custom ASIC thesis (Google TPU, Meta training, OpenAI/ByteDance inference). Volume confirmed the move. The R:R at current price is 0.2:1 — deeply unfavourable. Note tight_upside: nearest resistance is $401.39 (1.2% above), with the meaningful ceiling at $412.95 (4.1% above). PEG of 0.83 with 29.5% revenue growth at 22.0x forward PE is genuinely cheap on a growth-adjusted basis — the best blended hardware+software valuation in the semis cluster. FCF yield of 1.36% is thin. The VMware software layer adds recurring revenue durability, differentiating Broadcom from pure-play semis. No entry case at current levels — this requires a meaningful pullback to $334-$369 before the setup improves. Watch for whether the Meta partnership extension comes with firm revenue terms vs. a framework extension.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +20.1% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum at $824.01 fell 3.18% today and is down 8.04% over five sessions — consistent with profit-taking after an extraordinary 1,539% one-year run per Barchart. At 20.1% above its 50-day average of $685.90 (hard block), with a negative FCF yield of -0.04%, this is technically and fundamentally blocked for new entry. The PEG of 0.14 appears anomalous — with a 52x forward PE, implied earnings growth would need to be >350x to produce a 0.14 PEG, which suggests the metric is distorted by near-zero current earnings rather than representing genuine growth value. The R:R is 0.3:1 (16.8% downside to SMA50 vs. 5.0% upside to $865.21 resistance). The structural thesis — NVIDIA's confirmed $2B strategic investment (per NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) for AI optics and co-packaged optical interconnects — remains intact and is a multi-year demand driver. Aletheia Capital raised its target to $1,225 per Insider Monkey. Structural support at $642.37 is 22.0% below — that's the level where a genuine re-entry conversation begins. The sell-off is orderly, not a thesis break.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 76x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir at $142.15 gained 4.65% today but remains below its declining 50-day average of $143.61 (RSI 48.4, neutral territory). The entry block is a soft caution (base_scorer) due to the 76.3x forward PE — justification requires explicit earnings growth catalysts. The Q1 earnings print (May 5) is the critical validator: the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — not firm revenue) and the confirmed $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) need to translate into revenue guidance. Rosenblatt raised its target to $200, Daiwa upgraded to Buy at $180, per analyst notes. The 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY) is the justification for the multiple, but 76x forward PE versus a cluster median of 25.6x — a 198% premium — requires flawless execution. R:R of 0.5:1 to the $144.86 resistance is poor; structural support at $128.51 is 9.6% below. PEG of 2.68 and FCF yield of 0.37% place this firmly in caution_trim territory. Bottom line: the defense AI story is real, but the valuation prices in near-perfection — avoid until after May 5 earnings validate the guide.

WRD.entry_block: BLOCKED: +12.2% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=11 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 61 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

WRD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — WeRide recovered 2.16% today and has gained 3.98% over five sessions, showing stabilization after the prior sell-off. The stock sits 12.2% above its 50-day average of $7.22 — still hard-blocked for entry — but the trend direction has improved from the CAUTION thesis. RSI at 60.8 is neutral-bullish. The R:R at current price is poor (0.2:1), with nearest resistance at $8.29 (2.3% above) versus 10.9% downside to the SMA50 invalidation. The fundamental thesis — pre-profit robotaxi with $98M revenue, RMB7.1B cash, partnerships with Uber, Grab, Bosch across 8 countries — depends on commercial traction (fleet scale, revenue per vehicle, geographic expansion), not technology. Revenue growth of 123% is the most compelling number on the watchlist, but this is a speculative ADR with China delisting risk as a standing thesis invalidator — always a consideration. The SMA50 is not rising, suggesting the underlying trend hasn't turned. Wait for a pullback to the $7.22-$7.51 support zone before this becomes actionable — or for the 50-day average to flatten and begin rising, confirming a trend change.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains uniformly WATCH/CAUTION with US10Y at 4.28% — above the 4.20% NIM reset threshold — and the MAS maintaining its SGD appreciation pace on April 14, sustaining dual headwinds. OCBC is the standout mover, surging to a hard block at 5.81% above its SMA50 with RSI 68, making it the most extended and least attractive entry of the three. The key divergence: UOB trades at a meaningful 18.9% discount to the cluster median PE but carries a declining earnings consensus, while DBS trades at a slight 9.4% premium with the worst earnings decline forecast — neither offers a compelling entry at current prices.
singapore.key_development: MAS April 14 policy hold (SGD NEER appreciation maintained) extends the dual-headwind regime through at least October 2026.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX at $401.86 (-0.05%) is essentially flat today as the cluster digests ASML's post-earnings 3% drop on a Q2 revenue miss and TSMC's earnings report (due today per Reuters, expected 50% profit surge). The bifurcation persists: MU and TSMC have rising SMA50s (supply-chain isolation thesis intact), while NVDA and AMD are at extreme extensions (9% and 23% above SMA50 respectively) with deteriorating R:R at current prices. AVGO's Meta custom ASIC partnership extension is the day's key positive catalyst for the cluster's longer-term custom silicon vs. merchant GPU narrative.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcated — foundry supply-chain strength vs. mega-cap extension risk
semiconductors.key_development: TSMC Q1 earnings today (April 16) — 50% profit surge expected per Reuters; result will set the tone for the CoWoS capacity and AI chip demand narrative across the cluster.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster is uniformly extended and hard-blocked, with MSFT triggering a WATCH→CAUTION signal change after today's 4.26% surge pushed RSI above 65. All four members (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT, PLTR) are 5%+ above their 50-day averages with RSI readings of 67-76 — the setup is that the risk-on regime has run ahead of the April 30 earnings catalyst (AMZN, GOOG, MSFT all report that date). PLTR is the outlier: below its SMA50 but at 76x forward PE, needing May 5 earnings to justify the multiple.
big_tech.thesis_status: Extended and earnings-dependent — April 30 is the inflection
big_tech.key_development: MSFT surged 4.26% on US-Iran peace negotiation optimism, triggering RSI hard block — April 30 earnings (focus: non-OpenAI Azure RPO and organic cloud demand) is now the primary re-entry catalyst.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp
interconnected.reason: Received NVIDIA's matching $2B strategic investment alongside LITE for AI optics and co-packaged interconnects — directly linked to the same datacenter bandwidth bottleneck thesis driving LITE's watchlist position.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE pulls back 15-20% toward $685 SMA50 support, as both names tend to trade together on photonics catalyst days — entry into COHR becomes more interesting at the same relative point.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM — already on watchlist)
interconnected.reason: TSMC earnings today (April 16) are the direct CoWoS capacity read-through for NVDA, AMD, and AVGO — confirming or questioning whether the AI chip supply chain can sustain the hyperscaler capex narrative.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes actionable at $352-$363 (the 50-day average zone) if today's earnings deliver strong Q2 guidance — use the print as the re-entry trigger rather than the pre-earnings price.

interconnected.name: Arm Holdings
interconnected.reason: Arm's architecture underlies the custom AI chips being designed by AVGO (for Google, Meta) and NVDA's Grace CPU roadmap — the custom ASIC ramp directly drives Arm royalty revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after AMD and NVDA earnings (May 6 and May 21) for confirmation that custom silicon deployments are accelerating, which would pull forward Arm's royalty inflection.

interconnected.name: Applied Materials
interconnected.reason: ASML's Q2 revenue miss signals potential order softness in leading-edge equipment — AMAT as the adjacent deposition/etch equipment supplier gives a second read on whether the equipment capex cycle is peaking or merely digesting.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AMAT pulls back 10-12% from current levels on the ASML Q2 miss read-through, provided TSMC's earnings today confirm AI capacity expansion is still on track.

interconnected.name: Super Micro Computer
interconnected.reason: Directly benefits from NVDA Blackwell Ultra ramp as a key server integration partner — NVDA's 11-session win streak and confirmed $78B Q1 guide implies Blackwell is shipping faster than expected, which pulls forward SMCI rack integration volumes.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist only if VIX stays below 20 and SMCI pulls back 8-10% from current levels — accounting fraud concerns (2024 audit issues) require a larger margin of safety before entry.

active_risks: US Navy Hormuz blockade enters day 4 — Iranian ports blocked, WTI holding $91 after $104 spike; Iran threatens retaliation against military vessels in strait
US-Iran nuclear talks collapsed over weekend; blockade implementation began Monday at 10am ET per CENTCOM statement
US-China semiconductor supply chain: Section 122 tariffs (15% surcharges, 150 days) active; China 34% retaliatory tariffs + export controls on rare earths remain in force
Fed holding rates at 3.50-3.75% with US10Y at 4.28%; April CPI is the binary validator for transient vs. structural inflation from Hormuz energy shock
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz premium is being partially discounted — WTI at $91 is $13 below the blockade-day spike — but logistics rerouting costs remain structurally embedded in ASML and TSM supply chains, keeping both in CAUTION despite otherwise improving fundamentals. PLTR continues to benefit from defense AI spending tailwinds, but the 76x forward PE already prices in significant contract wins — the caution_trim designation reflects valuation, not thesis.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: TSMC Q1 earnings report released today (April 16) — per Reuters, expected 50% profit surge; result represents the first major datapoint on whether AI chip demand can sustain hyperscaler capex through the Hormuz logistics headwind.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective entry only in the $352-$363 TSM zone post-earnings; hold cash on extended names (NVDA, AMD, AMZN, AVGO) and await the April 30 BigTech earnings cluster before adding any new BigTech exposure.

events_this_week.event: TSMC Q1 2026 Earnings — expected 50% profit surge (per Reuters); CoWoS capacity and Q2 revenue guidance are the key metrics
Intel Q1 2026 Earnings — first post-18A-announcement print; loss trajectory and foundry customer pipeline commentary are the thesis validators
Amazon Q1 2026 Earnings — AWS revenue trajectory and capex-to-earnings conversion timeline are the key metrics; $200B capex overhang makes Q1 operating income critical
Alphabet (GOOG) Q1 2026 Earnings — TPU v7 rental revenue from Meta deal, cloud growth, and capex guidance are the pivotal datapoints
Microsoft Q1 FY2026 Earnings — focus on non-OpenAI Azure RPO growth (~$350B, +28%) and Azure ex-OpenAI commentary to assess organic cloud demand vs. credit-inflated headline numbers
DBS Group Q1 2026 Earnings — NIM trajectory and management commentary on US10Y sensitivity are the key WATCH upgrade conditions

Palantir Q1 2026 Earnings — $7.18-7.20B FY2026 revenue guide validation; DoD contract pipeline (Army IDIQ framework, Navy ShipOS) and commercial AI revenue are the binary catalysts that could resolve CAUTION signal
AMD Q1 2026 Earnings — MI450 shipment timeline and Meta framework activation commentary are the key signals for whether the custom silicon thesis can support the 23% SMA50 extension

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Seven tickers are in CAUTION — primarily due to hard entry blocks from extreme extensions above their 50-day averages — while the Hormuz blockade and elevated US10Y (4.28%) sustain dual headwinds across the Singapore bank cluster and semi supply chains; no actionable entries exist until either names pull back to SMA50 zones or the April 30 earnings cluster provides a catalyst.

watch_for_entry.note: Nearest resistance just 0.2% above current price — no upside room. Actionable on pullback to SGD 56.30-56.41 SMA50 support with volume, or on US10Y breaking sustainably below 4.20%.

Cheapest Singapore bank at 10.3x forward PE (18.9% discount to cluster). Actionable at SGD 35.92 structural support or on US10Y break below 4.20%. Sizing R:R to structural support is 1.3:1 — below conviction threshold.
Speculative ADR — 123% revenue growth but pre-profit with China delisting risk. Actionable only on pullback to $7.22-$7.51 SMA50 zone with SMA50 flattening. Half position at best given ADR risk.

caution_trim.note: Avoid. Hard-blocked at 5.81% above SMA50 with RSI 68 — extended after strong run; NIM compression thesis unresolved with US10Y at 4.28%.
Avoid at current levels. 9.0% above SMA50 with RSI 70; R:R 0.8:1 at current price. Support at $177.41 is the re-entry level.
Avoid. Q2 revenue guidance miss post-earnings; 6.1% above SMA50, PEG 2.49 at 32.4x forward PE flags expensive-for-growth at this extension.
Avoid ahead of and immediately after Q1 earnings today. Binary event risk; 6.5% above SMA50. Re-evaluate at $352-$363 SMA50 zone after the earnings dust settles.
Avoid — RSI hard block triggered at 67.3 after 4.26% surge. Wait for pullback to $391-$395 SMA50 zone before April 30 earnings.
Avoid. 20.1% extreme extension, negative FCF yield, anomalous PEG. Re-entry conversation starts near $642-$685 structural support zone only.
Avoid. 76x forward PE at 198% premium to cluster median — valuation requires flawless execution. Wait for May 5 earnings to validate $7.18-7.20B revenue guide.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Coherent Corp
new_stocks_to_watch.note: NVIDIA's matching $2B investment partner alongside LITE for AI optics. S&P 500 member. Directly linked to the co-packaged optics bottleneck thesis. Scouting only — no technical data available.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE pulls back 15-20% toward $685 SMA50 — both names move together on photonics catalyst days, creating a more attractive relative entry point.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Arm Holdings
new_stocks_to_watch.note: Architecture royalties benefit directly from AVGO custom ASIC ramp (Google TPU, Meta chips) and NVDA Grace CPU. Clean royalty model with no fab exposure.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Revisit after AMD earnings on May 6 — if custom silicon deployment commentary confirms accelerating volumes, Arm's royalty inflection timeline pulls forward, justifying watchlist addition.

new_stocks_to_watch.name: Applied Materials
new_stocks_to_watch.note: Adjacent semiconductor equipment read — ASML's Q2 revenue miss raises questions about whether leading-edge equipment capex is peaking. AMAT gives a second datapoint on the equipment cycle.
new_stocks_to_watch.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AMAT pulls back 10-12% from current levels on the ASML Q2 miss read-through AND TSMC's earnings today confirm AI capacity expansion remains on track.

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: 50%+ profit confirmed with strong Q2 revenue guidance and CoWoS capacity expansion commentary
bullish_upgrades: TSM → WATCH candidate on pullback to $352-$363
NVDA → caution eases, support at $177 becomes cleaner re-entry
MU → HOLD confirmed, SMA50 pullback becomes more attractive
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Q2 guidance disappoints on logistics cost inflation or CoWoS capacity constraints
bearish_impact: TSM → CAUTION deepens, structural support at $330.99 becomes the reference
NVDA → thesis weakens on supply-side constraint signals
ASML → Q2 miss narrative compounds, no upgrade path near-term

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: 18A external customer announcement or better-than-expected loss trajectory
bullish_upgrades: INTC → WATCH candidate, first external 18A win would be a thesis catalyst
ASML → High-NA EUV Intel 14A ramp timeline reinforced
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: No external 18A customers confirmed, guidance cut or widening losses
bearish_impact: INTC → HOLD maintained, extreme 34.7% extension becomes pure sentiment risk
ASML → High-NA EUV ramp timeline pushed further right

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: AWS re-acceleration, GOOG TPU v7 revenue materializing, MSFT non-OpenAI Azure RPO growing >30%
bullish_upgrades: AMZN → WATCH if price pulls back to $213-226 zone pre-earnings
GOOG → WATCH on pullback to $307-319 with TPU revenue confirmation
MSFT → WATCH to BUY candidate if RSI normalizes below 60 by April 30
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Capex guidance maintained or raised without operating income inflection; Azure growth disappoints ex-OpenAI
bearish_impact: AMZN → HOLD deepens, $213 SMA50 becomes critical floor
GOOG → potential CAUTION on institutional distribution re-emergence
MSFT → CAUTION sustained, OpenAI concentration risk re-surfaces in analyst commentary
